== IO ==

* AutoCloseable.close can throw any exception, whereas Closeable.close can only throw IOException
Closeable interface extends AutoCloseable

* Print Writers are always buffered. We can enable autoflushing explicitily.
* You should not use the `File` class except to interface with legacy code. (Instead use `Path`)
* There is a small risk with serializing inner classes if you intend to have them deserialized with a different Java implementation. Static inner classes, including inner enumerations and records, are safe to serialize.
* Never call overridable methods from constructor nor from readObject() of Serailizable classes.
[[index]] -- Home
